memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Darrell, Dorrell, Dayrell, Darrel, Darell, Darel and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from very early times and were granted lands by Duke William of Normandy, their liege Lord, for their distinguished assistance at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: John Darrell who settled in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1637 and moved to Salem; Moses and Mary Darrell settled in Virginia in 1654; William Darrell settled in Virginia in 1643. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Recorded in various spellings including Darell, Darrel, Darrell, Dearl, Dearle, Derell, Derl, Durlle, and the more popular Durrell, this unusual surname is usually medieval English, but of Norman-French origins. It is locational from the place called "Airel" in the La Manche region of Normandy. The place name means "the courtyard", derived from the early Latin word "arealis", describing an open space and curiously is itself a derivative of the word "area", used in the original sense to mean a threshing floor. The surname is formed from by fusing the French preposition "de", meaning "of" to the place name of Airel. Presumably the surname was introduced into England after the Norman Conquest of the country in 1066, and over the centuries the surname development has included Dearle in Norfolk in 1563, Darrell in London in 1580, Dearell in Hampshire in 1585, and many others. Early examples of the surname recordings taken from surviving church registers of the diocese of Greater London include: Flora Durrell, a witness at St Johns Hackney, on 11 November 1623, the marriage of William Dearle to Ann Trafman at St. Mary le Bone, Marylebone, on 20 May 1677, and James Derl, the son of James and Maria Derl, who was christened at St Leonard's Shoreditch, on 11 September 1842. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of William Darell. This was dated 1206, at Sessay, in North Yorkshire, during the reign of King John of England, 1199 - 1216. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Darell於1384年出生在英國越可爾瑟賽時，他的父親Sir William Darrell25歲，母親Lady Emma d'Aubigny21歲。他於大約1415年與Elizabeth de Calston在英國維爾爾利特萊可特 白 伍頓 巴塞特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1461年3月14日享年77歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾拉姆斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Darell于1384年出生在英国越可爾瑟賽时，他的父亲Sir William Darrell25岁，母亲Lady Emma d'Aubigny21岁。他于大约1415年与Elizabeth de Calston在英国維爾爾利特萊可特 白 伍頓 巴塞特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1461年3月14日享年77岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾拉姆斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Darell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Darell was born in 1384, in Sessay, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir William Darrell, was 25 and his mother, Lady Emma d'Aubigny, was 21. He married Elizabeth de Calston about 1415, in Littlecott by Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 14 March 1461, at the age of 77, and was buried in Ramsbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Elizabeth de Calston environ 1415, à Littlecott by Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 14 mars 1461, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Darell nasceu em 1384, em Sessay, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Darrell, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Emma d'Aubigny, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Calston aproximadamente 1415, em Littlecott by Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de março de 1461, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Ramsbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Littlecott by Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire: литлекотт би вуттон бассетт, уилцхир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Darell родился в 1384 г. в местности сессеи, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Darrell было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Emma d'Aubigny, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Calston около 1415 г. в местности литлекотт би вуттон бассетт, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 14 марта 1461 г. в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности рамсбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.
